score_rationale: Solid relevance to user_01's factory worker profile — references layoff event, frugal living, remittances home. Cites specific numbers (emotion mean 0.52, savings rate 46.3%, calorie 1340kcal, sleep 6.9h, anomalous spend 1222 yuan on 3/15). Cross-domain connections are present (emotion-diet loop, financial shock vs emotional recovery, cheap diet vs high savings paradox) though some causal chains are speculative and over-interpreted (e.g., claiming zero late-night eating is 'restraint against anxiety' rather than simply normal behavior). Temporal drift analysis attempts to track pre/post job change but stretches thin data. Actionability is the weakest dimension — recommendations are cut off mid-sentence and the completed portions are generic (eat healthier, set budget red lines). For a 2B model, this is above-average: it maintains structural coherence and attempts genuine cross-domain reasoning rather than just listing per-app summaries.
